sections.paragraph:
διάστημα, ατος, τό (Pla. et al.; pap, LXX; GrBar 2:4; EpArist, Philo, Joseph.; Ar. 4:2; Ath., R. 68, 5.—D in Ac 5:7 spells it διάστεμα [B-D-F §109, 3 app.]) a space of time between events, interval ἐγένετο ὡς ὡρῶν τριῶν δ. after an interval of about three hours Ac 5:7 (cp. Aristot., Aud. 800b, 5; Polyb. 9, 1, 1 τετραετὲς δ.; Philo; PParis 1, 381; PGiss 40 II, 15 μετὰ τὸ πληρωθῆναι τὸ τοῦ χρόνου διάστημα); δ. ποιεῖν (Gen 32:17) leave an interval B 9:8.—DELG s.v. ἵστημι. M-M. Sv.

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

app. app. = appendix, apparatus (when ref. to marginal text-critical information, esp. in N.)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

PParis PParis = Notices et textes des papyrus (Paris)—List 4

PGiss PGiss = Griechische Papyri . . . zu Giessen—List 4

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 237
